text: We have found that Zevo's are not effective. I had the same question a month ago and we bought a BuzBug indoor bug zapper that was recommended. And this thing works very well. Obviously can't be in the enclosure, but able to set it near our Guineas enclosure.

text: Is Rufus’ cage near the kitchen? It might just be the usual fruit flies that are attracted to his veggies? Or maybe you got some hay with eggs on it and they hatched? I haven’t come across that problem myself but I’d say check those things and that plug in could be helpful. Super cute video🥰

text: So sorry to hear. 😕 It’s a tough choice. Is there any chance of her passing the stone? I struggled with the same decision. Talking to my vet I opted for palliative care. My little guy had a decent sized (4mm) stone, but shockingly he was able to pass it a month later. Unfortunately when they’ve had bladder stones they’re more likely to develop more in the future which is what I’m currently dealing with. I’m hoping he can pass them but it’s very hard. I’m so strict with his diet but if they’re predisposed it’s hard to prevent them.

text: I used 18” panels to allow enough clearance for the loft and I pad steel bars across the bottom to make it extra sturdy. If you do add a loft, make sure you have a ramp that is long enough so as not to make it too steep. Piggies can get injured if the ramp is too steep. Always make sure your ramps are covered with some type of fabric to create traction.

text: Can you post a clear picture of tooth? Has he been having difficulty eating? You may need to compensate by offering him syringe food. You should be able to get Critical Care and 1mL feeding syringes from your veterinarian. Critical Care can also be ordered online. It will be necessary to offer feedings to the unaffected side as it might be painful on the side where the broken tooth sits. Do you find him still showing interest in food, or has there been any change in appetite since starting antibiotic?
Their teeth grow in two weeks or so
Pack n play for the single and a Playyard for the pair. You can get them used on Facebook fairly cheap!
Get a small pop out tent from Amazon
Switch to orchard grass hay as its softer.  Oxbow orchard grass hay is what i buy.  Available online or at Petsmart or wherever you go to get piggie food.

text: Get rid of the hay bag and either place a big pile of hay on the floor or buy a litter box. They poop where they eat and they also enjoy being in the hay. Just a suggestion for happy pigs.

attachments.ocrText: May be an image of baby and toy

text: For sure get rid of the hay bag! Everything else looks awesome. The hay bags have caused many strangulation hazards in the past year you could probably take it back to the pet store and swap it out for something else

text: There are lots of guinea pig rescues in Kent. Rescues get young ones as well and always better to rescue if possible 😊
That's a pig for you!! 70 years of pigs, I could tell ya a lot!!!

attachments.ocrText: No photo description available.

text: A washing basket. Not the dirty clothes one, the one you use to take the clean washing to the clothes line. Why? You might ask. Have some treats that they like, place a hand towel in the basket to catch any accidents. Place both piggies in the basket and sit with it on your lap. You can now give both attention and treats at the same time. This will show that you love them equally. Gently pat alternately. While patting one, give the other a small treat and then switch. Both together with you with no favouritism. Hopefully they will realise that you are part of the herd and not just their butler and maid.😉. I am no professional. I am simply offering something that worked for me. Good luck. It's not fail proof, as my male (neutered) expects the last pat and a good night pat. He was my first and we bonded originally, without the 2 girls in the herd. I hope this might give you ideas that work for you.
